filtered_findings.description: Variable fallback_used is referenced but never defined in the function scope.
filtered_findings.recommendation: Remove the conditional logic that references fallback_used or define the variable properly.
filtered_findings.rationale: This will cause a NameError at runtime when the condition is true.

filtered_findings.description: Exception caught but not logged or handled. Makes debugging difficult.
filtered_findings.recommendation: Add logging or re-raise with context.
filtered_findings.rationale: Silent exceptions hide bugs and make troubleshooting impossible.

filtered_findings.description: Exception caught but not logged or handled.
filtered_findings.recommendation: Add logging or re-raise with context.

filtered_findings.description: Exception caught but not logged in git state capture.
filtered_findings.recommendation: Add logging to capture git errors.
filtered_findings.rationale: Git state capture failures should be logged for debugging.

filtered_findings.issue: Debug logging code should be conditional or removed
filtered_findings.description: Lines 823-846 contain debug logging that writes all input data to disk. This may leak sensitive information in production.
filtered_findings.recommendation: Make debug logging conditional on DEBUG flag or remove.
filtered_findings.rationale: Logging all input data may expose sensitive user information.

filtered_findings.issue: Debug logging code should be conditional or removed
filtered_findings.description: Lines 441-460 contain debug logging that writes all input data to disk.
filtered_findings.recommendation: Make debug logging conditional on DEBUG flag or remove.
filtered_findings.rationale: Logging all input data may expose sensitive user information.

rejected_findings.description: Using __import__("threading") instead of direct import. This is unusual pattern.
rejected_findings.recommendation: Replace with direct import: import threading
rejected_findings.rationale: Direct imports are more readable and standard. __import__ is typically used for dynamic module loading which is not needed here.
rejected_findings.reason: Layer 4.5: Dynamic __import__ is unusual but not a security vulnerability. Direct import would be clearer but this is not exploitable.

rejected_findings.issue: Test coverage badge shows 35% which is low for production code
rejected_findings.description: README badge shows coverage: 35% (yellow). Target should be >80% per CLAUDE.md guidelines.
rejected_findings.recommendation: Increase test coverage to >80% or update badge if inaccurate.
rejected_findings.rationale: Low test coverage increases risk of regressions.
rejected_findings.reason: Layer 4.5: Test coverage is a metric, not a code defect. Addressing it requires new tests, not fixing existing code.
